backlog.title: Bugfix 002 — Startup Preflight Placement & Clean Harness Error Path
backlog.description: Fix the two narrow startup-path defects identified in delta 007 (D1) adversarial validation. (1) MAJOR: the fail-fast auth preflight (PRD §9.2.7) blocks the pure-local CLI modes `--validate-prd` and `--dry-run` that make zero API calls. (2) MINOR: `PRP_AGENT_HARNESS=claude-code` + default `zai` models throws `HarnessProviderMismatchError` at MODULE-EVAL time (via `agent-factory.ts`'s top-level `configureHarness()`), surfacing as an uncaught raw stack trace instead of the clean one-liner the §9.2.7 preflight was added to provide. Both fixes target `src/index.ts` `main()` startup ordering and the module-eval side effect in `src/agents/agent-factory.ts`. The auth resolution, logging teardown, and cross-repo Groundswell contract are verified SOLID and are explicitly OUT of scope. See architecture/system_context.md for the root-cause analysis and final ordering.

milestones.title: Startup Path Corrections (preflight placement + clean harness error)
milestones.description: Reorder `main()` so pure-local CLI modes run credential-free before the auth preflight/harness init, and convert the `agent-factory.ts` module-load `configureHarness()` into a lazy accessor surfaced cleanly on the `main()` startup path. Both changes touch the same region of `main()`, so they are sequenced (Issue 1 first, then Issue 2) to avoid conflicts.

tasks.title: Issue 1 (MAJOR): Exempt pure-local CLI modes (--validate-prd, --dry-run) from the auth preflight
tasks.description: `--validate-prd` (local PRD markdown lint via `PRDValidator`, which imports only `prd-differ`/`node:fs`/`node:path`) and `--dry-run` (prints the plan and returns 0) make ZERO API calls, yet `main()` runs `runAuthPreflight()` + `ensureHarnessInitialized()` unconditionally before their early-return branches — so a no-credential run aborts with exit 1 and a misleading 'pi /login / export ZAI_API_KEY' message. Fix by moving the two local-only early-returns (and the root logger creation) to BEFORE `runAuthPreflight()`/`ensureHarnessInitialized()`, preserving every §9.2.7 acceptance criterion for the agent-invoking paths (which are the only paths the preflight's motivation — 'not detected until the first agent calls the model' — applies to).

subtasks.title: Reorder main(): local-only early-returns before preflight + harness init (move root logger up)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/system_context.md §1 (current `main()` flow) and §2 (Issue 1 root cause + fix) and §4 (final ordering). The bugfix PRD §h3.2 'Suggested fix' gives the exact target ordering. The current order in `src/index.ts` `main()` is: `configureEnvironment()` → `await runAuthPreflight()` → `await ensureHarnessInitialized()` → `const logger = getLogger('App', {verbose, machineReadable})` → `if (args.dryRun){...return 0}` → `if (args.validatePrd){...return result.valid?0:1}` → `new PRPPipeline(...)`. The two local-only modes are gated by the preflight despite making zero API calls.
2. INPUT: `ValidatedCLIArgs` from `parseCLIArgs()` (src/cli/index.ts) — fields `dryRun: boolean` (line 76), `validatePrd: boolean` (line 94), `prd: string`, `verbose`, `machineReadable`. `PRDValidator` from `src/utils/prd-validator.ts` (already local-only — imports ONLY prd-differ/node:fs/node:path; do NOT modify it). The existing `getLogger`, `runAuthPreflight`, `ensureHarnessInitialized` imports are unchanged.
3. LOGIC: In `src/index.ts` `main()`, reorder to: `configureEnvironment()` → `const logger = getLogger('App', {verbose: args.verbose, machineReadable: args.machineReadable})` (move the existing logger creation UP to immediately after configureEnvironment; it is independent of creds/harness and is already lazy w.r.t. PRD §9.6.2 REQ-L2 because it lives inside main(), not at module top-level) → `if (args.dryRun){...}` early-return 0 → `if (args.validatePrd){...}` early-return (result.valid?0:1) → `await runAuthPreflight()` → `await ensureHarnessInitialized()` → pipeline. Keep the BODIES of the dry-run and validate-prd blocks BYTE-FOR-BYTE identical to today (only their POSITION changes). Do NOT change `runAuthPreflight`/`ensureHarnessInitialized`/`PRDValidator`/`parseCLIArgs`. NOTE: `--help`/`-h`/`--version`/`-V`/unknown-flag/`inspect` already call `process.exit()` inside `parseCLIArgs()` BEFORE `main()`'s body, so they are unaffected by this reorder — verify they still short-circuit.
4. OUTPUT: `src/index.ts` `main()` where `--dry-run`/`--validate-prd` return BEFORE any credential/harness requirement, consumed by P1.M1.T1.S2 (acceptance tests) and P1.M1.T2.S2 (which inserts the explicit `configureHarness()` call into the gap between the early-returns and `runAuthPreflight()`).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the JSDoc `@remarks` block on the `main()` function in `src/index.ts` to document the new startup ordering — explicitly state that pure-local modes (`--dry-run`, `--validate-prd`) run credential-free BEFORE the §9.2.7 preflight and harness initialization, and cite bugfix PRD §h3.2. This rides WITH the code change; do not create a separate docs subtask.

subtasks.title: Subprocess acceptance tests: --validate-prd & --dry-run succeed with NO credential
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/test-conventions.md §4 'Pattern A' (the `spawnSync(process.execPath, [CLI, ...])` + scrubbed `env` + `describeOrSkip = existsSync(CLI) ? describe : describe.skip` pattern) and architecture/system_context.md §6 (coverage reality for src/index.ts). Mirror `tests/unit/config/auth-preflight.test.ts`'s acceptance `(a)` block EXACTLY for: the `AUTH_VARS` delete-list (ZAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_TOKEN, PRP_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL, PRP_AGENT_HARNESS), the scrubbed `env` object (only PATH/HOME/USER/SHELL + PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR), `PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR` → fresh `mkdtempSync` empty dir, and the `plan/NNN_*` session-dir before/after diff (regex /^\d{3}_[0-9a-f]{12}$/). The vitest `resolve.alias.groundswell` + `vi.mock('groundswell')` MASK module-load behavior, so subprocess is the ONLY way to prove the real CLI runs credential-free.
2. INPUT: The reordered `main()` from P1.M1.T1.S1. The built `dist/index.js` (resolve(process.cwd(),'dist/index.js')); guard with existsSync.
3. LOGIC: Add a build-guarded subprocess suite asserting BOTH modes run credential-free: (a) `hack --prd <abs PRD.md> --validate-prd` with scrubbed env (empty PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR, NO credential env vars) → `res.status === 0`, stdout/stderr contains the validation report with '✅ VALID' (PRD.md in repo root is valid), AND no new `plan/NNN_*` session dir created (readdir before/after diff equal). (b) `hack --prd <abs PRD.md> --dry-run` with the SAME scrubbed env → `res.status === 0`, output contains 'DRY RUN', AND no new session dir created. CRITICAL: assert the command does NOT print 'Authentication preflight failed' (proving it bypassed the preflight). Prefer extending the existing `tests/unit/config/auth-preflight.test.ts` acceptance describe (keeps related subprocess coverage together) OR a new `tests/unit/cli/local-modes.test.ts` — match the repo's existing organization (no groundswell vi.mock needed for a pure-subprocess file, but include the AUTH_VARS hygiene if you add any in-process setup). COVERAGE NOTE: subprocess runs do NOT feed the v8 in-process instrumenter; src/index.ts is at 0% in the committed coverage report and that is pre-existing — do NOT chase 100% on index.ts and do NOT add istanbul-ignore. The subprocess assertions are the authoritative acceptance.
4. OUTPUT: Passing tests proving `--validate-prd`/`--dry-run` are credential-free end-to-end on the built CLI.
5. DOCS: none — test-only change; no user-facing/config/API surface change.

tasks.title: Issue 2 (MINOR): Clean error for harness/provider mismatch (no module-load crash)
tasks.description: `configureHarness()` is invoked at MODULE-EVAL time via `const RESOLVED_HARNESS = configureHarness()` at the top of `src/agents/agent-factory.ts`, which is in the static import graph of `dist/index.js` (via DeltaAnalysisWorkflow/BugHuntWorkflow → PRPPipeline → index.ts). For `PRP_AGENT_HARNESS=claude-code` + default `zai` provider it throws `HarnessProviderMismatchError` BEFORE `main()` runs, so `main().catch()` never catches it and Node prints a raw stack trace + 'Node.js v...' banner. The combination IS genuinely invalid (claude-code is Anthropic-only — PRD §9.4.3/§9.2.4); only the error FORM is the defect. Fix (bugfix PRD §h3.3 Preferred option): (a) make `RESOLVED_HARNESS` lazy in `agent-factory.ts` (mirroring the REQ-L2 logger accessor already in that file) so module load no longer throws; (b) invoke `configureHarness()` explicitly on the `main()` startup path AFTER the local-only early-returns (in the gap created by Issue 1) and BEFORE `runAuthPreflight()`, so its error renders via the clean handler.

subtasks.title: Lazy-ify RESOLVED_HARNESS in agent-factory.ts (remove module-eval configureHarness() side effect)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/system_context.md §1 (static import graph: index.ts→PRPPipeline→DeltaAnalysisWorkflow/BugHuntWorkflow→agent-factory.ts, all STATIC) and §3 (Issue 2 root cause + fix). The bugfix PRD §h3.3 Preferred option mandates mirroring 'the REQ-L2 logger pattern'. That pattern is ALREADY in agent-factory.ts: `let _logger: Logger | undefined; const logger = (): Logger => (_logger ??= getLogger('AgentFactory'));`. `configureHarness()` (src/config/harness.ts) is safe to call more than once — the `HarnessRegistry.has('pi')` guard prevents double-registration and `configureHarnesses()` is a config-singleton setter. NOTE: `agent-factory.ts` ALSO calls `configureEnvironment()` at module top-level (immediately above RESOLVED_HARNESS); fold THAT into the same lazy accessor (configureEnvironment() must run before configureHarness()).
2. INPUT: `configureHarness` and `configureEnvironment` already imported in src/agents/agent-factory.ts (from ../config/harness.js and ../config/environment.js). `createBaseConfig(persona)` currently reads `harness: RESOLVED_HARNESS`.
3. LOGIC: In `src/agents/agent-factory.ts`, REMOVE the two module-eval side effects (`configureEnvironment();` and `const RESOLVED_HARNESS: AgentHarness = configureHarness();` near the top). Replace with a memoized lazy accessor that calls configureEnvironment() then configureHarness() on first invocation and caches: `let _resolvedHarness: AgentHarness | undefined; const resolvedHarness = (): AgentHarness => { if (_resolvedHarness === undefined) { configureEnvironment(); _resolvedHarness = configureHarness(); } return _resolvedHarness; };`. Update `createBaseConfig()` to read `harness: resolvedHarness()` instead of `harness: RESOLVED_HARNESS`. Keep everything else (MCP singletons, persona token limits, agent factory functions) unchanged. RESULT: importing index.ts no longer triggers configureHarness() at module load → no module-eval throw.
4. OUTPUT: `src/agents/agent-factory.ts` with zero module-eval side effects; `createBaseConfig` resolves the harness lazily on first agent creation. Consumed by P1.M1.T2.S2 (main() explicit call) and P1.M1.T2.S3 (subprocess test). The exported symbol surface is unchanged (no new exports; `RESOLVED_HARNESS` was module-local, not exported).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the `@remarks` JSDoc at the top of `src/agents/agent-factory.ts` (currently claims 'Environment configuration is performed at module load time') and the inline 'PATTERN: Configure environment at module load time (intentional side effect)' comment to state the harness is now resolved LAZILY via an accessor on first agent creation (not at module load), citing bugfix PRD §h3.3 and PRD §9.6.2 REQ-L2 lazy-accessor pattern. Rides WITH the code change.

subtasks.title: Explicit configureHarness() call in main() + extend main().catch() for HarnessProviderMismatchError
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/system_context.md §3 (Issue 2 fix step 2) and §4 (final ordering). The `main().catch()` handler at the bottom of `src/index.ts` currently special-cases ONLY `AuthPreflightError` (prints `❌ <message>` + process.exit(1)); everything else falls through to a generic 'Fatal error in main()' dump. `HarnessProviderMismatchError` (src/config/types.ts) already carries an actionable message (names harness+provider+§9.2.4+both remediations: switch harness to 'pi' OR switch model provider to anthropic/*), so it just needs the SAME clean rendering arm.
2. INPUT: The reordered `main()` from P1.M1.T1.S1 (local-only early-returns now precede the preflight). The lazy `resolvedHarness()` accessor from P1.M1.T2.S1. `configureHarness` already imported in src/index.ts (from ./config/harness.js). `HarnessProviderMismatchError` exported from src/config/types.ts.
3. LOGIC: (a) In `src/index.ts` `main()`, insert an explicit `configureHarness()` call in the GAP between the local-only early-returns (P1.M1.T1.S1) and `await runAuthPreflight()` — i.e. it runs only on agent-invoking paths, AFTER configureEnvironment() and BEFORE runAuthPreflight()/ensureHarnessInitialized(). This populates the global harness singleton + Groundswell config eagerly on the path that needs it, and makes P1.M1.T2.S1's lazy accessor a no-op cache hit when createBaseConfig later runs. Wrap it so its error is NOT fatal-dumped: the cleanest approach is (b) below. (b) Extend the `main().catch((error) => {...})` handler to ALSO special-case `HarnessProviderMismatchError` exactly like `AuthPreflightError`: `if (error instanceof HarnessProviderMismatchError) { console.error(\`\n❌ \${error.message}\`); process.exit(1); }` (add it as an additional `instanceof` arm, importing HarnessProviderMismatchError from ./config/types.js alongside AuthPreflightError). Add the import at the top. This guarantees a clean one-liner exit 1 even if the throw ever occurs on another path.
4. OUTPUT: `src/index.ts` main() where agent-invoking paths run `configureHarness()` (clean error on mismatch) before the preflight, and `main().catch()` renders HarnessProviderMismatchError as a friendly single message + exit 1. Consumed by P1.M1.T2.S3 (subprocess acceptance test). NOTE: keep behavior identical for valid configs (pi+zai default proceeds; configureHarness is idempotent).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Extend the `main()` JSDoc `@remarks` (updated in P1.M1.T1.S1) to note that `configureHarness()` is now invoked explicitly on the agent-path startup (after local-only early-returns, before the preflight) and that its mismatch error is rendered via the same clean handler as AuthPreflightError. Rides WITH the code change.

subtasks.title: Subprocess acceptance test: claude-code + zai → clean exit 1, NO raw stack trace
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/test-conventions.md §4 (subprocess spawnSync pattern, scrubbed env, describeOrSkip build guard) and §6, and architecture/system_context.md §6 (vitest alias/groundswell mock MASK module-load behavior → subprocess is mandatory). The bugfix PRD §h3.3 'Actual behavior' shows the exact bad output to suppress: a `file:///.../dist/config/harness.js` throw site, `at ModuleJob.run`, and a `Node.js v26.2.0` banner. The good output is the `HarnessProviderMismatchError` message: contains 'Harness', "'claude-code'", "'zai'", '§9.2.4', and BOTH remediations ('pi' and 'anthropic').
2. INPUT: The lazy `agent-factory.ts` (P1.M1.T2.S1) + the explicit `configureHarness()` call + extended `main().catch()` (P1.M1.T2.S2), built into `dist/index.js`.
3. LOGIC: Add a build-guarded subprocess suite (spawnSync(process.execPath, [CLI, '--prd', absPrd, '--dry-run'], {encoding:'utf8', timeout:20_000, env: scrubbedEnv})) where scrubbedEnv = {PATH, HOME, USER, SHELL, PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR→empty temp dir} PLUS `PRP_AGENT_HARNESS=claude-code` (and NO zai/anthropic credential, NO model override so provider stays default 'zai'). Assert: (a) `res.status === 1`; (b) stderr CONTAINS the friendly message fragments 'incompatible with provider' and 'Node.js v' is ABSENT (i.e. `expect(res.stderr).not.toContain('Node.js v')` and `.not.toContain('at ModuleJob.run')` and `.not.toContain('at file://')`); (c) stderr CONTAINS the actionable remediation ('PRP_AGENT_HARNESS=pi' or 'pi' and 'anthropic'); (d) no new `plan/NNN_*` session dir created. Also add a NEGATIVE/control assertion that the DEFAULT pi+zai config with a credential still reaches dry-run exit 0 (to prove the explicit configureHarness() call didn't break the happy path) — or rely on P1.M1.T1.S2's dry-run success test for that. Place alongside the other subprocess acceptance tests (auth-preflight.test.ts acceptance describe or a new cli/harness-error.test.ts). COVERAGE NOTE: subprocess does not feed in-process coverage for index.ts (pre-existing 0%); for the lazy `resolvedHarness()` accessor in agent-factory.ts, in-process coverage DOES apply and must cover the memoization branch (hit by any existing test that constructs an agent config, e.g. via createBaseConfig) — verify agent-factory.ts coverage is not reduced.
4. OUTPUT: Passing tests proving the invalid claude-code+zai config fails cleanly (exit 1, single message, no raw stack/banner) on the built CLI.
5. DOCS: none — test-only change; no user-facing/config/API surface change.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Mode B (final) sweep of cross-cutting/overview docs that describe the §9.2.7 preflight startup ordering or the --validate-prd/--dry-run CLI modes and would read stale after Issues 1 & 2: these modes are now credential-free, and an invalid claude-code+zai config now fails with a clean one-liner (not a stack trace). Per-file JSDoc on the changed symbols was already updated WITH the implementing subtasks (Mode A in P1.M1.T1.S1 / P1.M1.T2.S1 / P1.M1.T2.S2); this task covers ONLY the whole-feature/overview docs that span the entire changeset.

subtasks.title: Update README.md (Configuration + Troubleshooting) re: credential-free local modes & clean harness error
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/system_context.md §7 (documentation surface). README.md currently states (Configuration, ~line 242) 'A startup preflight aborts with an actionable error if none is present' and (Troubleshooting, ~line 372-378) describes the preflight abort. After this changeset those statements are accurate ONLY for agent-invoking paths; --validate-prd/--dry-run are now exempt.
2. INPUT: Verified behaviors from P1.M1.T1.S2 (--validate-prd/--dry-run exit 0 credential-free) and P1.M1.T2.S3 (claude-code+zai → clean exit 1).
3. LOGIC: In README.md: (a) in the Configuration / prerequisites note (~line 242) and/or the --validate-prd/--dry-run CLI table rows, clarify that the auth preflight (PRD §9.2.7) gates ONLY agent-invoking runs and that `--validate-prd` and `--dry-run` run WITHOUT any credential (they are pure-local). (b) In Troubleshooting, optionally add a short note that an invalid `PRP_AGENT_HARNESS=claude-code` + default z.ai configuration fails fast with a single actionable message (switch harness to `pi` OR switch to `anthropic/*` models) rather than a stack trace. Keep edits minimal and consistent with the existing tone; do NOT restate the full PRD.
4. OUTPUT: README.md consistent with the new startup behavior.
5. DOCS: this IS the docs task (Mode B).

subtasks.title: Update docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md, docs/CONFIGURATION.md, docs/INSTALLATION.md re: credential-free local modes & clean harness error
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/system_context.md §7. Verified grep hits: docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md (--dry-run ~line 195, --validate-prd ~line 205, exit-code section ~line 243); docs/CONFIGURATION.md (~line 127 'validates this at startup and fails fast', flag table ~line 209/214, auth note ~line 441); docs/INSTALLATION.md (onboarding sequence — users may validate a PRD before setting up auth).
2. INPUT: Verified behaviors from P1.M1.T1.S2 and P1.M1.T2.S3.
3. LOGIC: (a) docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md — in the --dry-run and --validate-prd descriptions (~lines 195/205), note these modes require NO API credential (pure-local); ensure the exit-code table (~line 243) is consistent (exit 0 valid / 1 invalid for --validate-prd is unchanged). (b) docs/CONFIGURATION.md — refine the 'validates this at startup and fails fast' note (~line 127) and the auth-required footnote (~line 44) so it is clear the preflight gates agent runs only; `--validate-prd`/`--dry-run` are exempt. (c) docs/INSTALLATION.md — in the onboarding/getting-started sequence, reflect that a new user can run `--validate-prd` to lint their PRD BEFORE configuring API access (this is the natural first step the bug restored). (d) Where a harness/provider mismatch error is mentioned (or an error-reference section exists), note it fails fast with a single actionable message for `PRP_AGENT_HARNESS=claude-code` + default z.ai. Keep edits surgical; do not reformat unrelated content.
4. OUTPUT: docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md, docs/CONFIGURATION.md, docs/INSTALLATION.md consistent with the new behavior.
